text: The Federal Office of Public Health (FOPH) is dedicated to the health of the Swiss population. It’s responsible for national healthcare policy and for health and accident insurance; and it promotes healthcare issues and interests on the international stage. Patient with a hantavirus infection being treated in hospital The person is currently being treated at the University Hospital Zurich (USZ).
source: Swiss Federal Office of Public Health

text: The patient is male and was travelling on the cruise ship on which there were a number of hantavirus cases. The USZ is prepared to deal with such cases, is able to care for the patient, and guarantee the safety of staff and all patients. There is currently no risk to the Swiss public.
source: Swiss Federal Office of Public Health

text: More about «Patient with a hantavirus infection being treated in hospital» The authorities, hospitals and other players are jointly working to ensure that the people injured in the devastating fire in Crans-Montana receive care and treatment and that their relatives are supported. You can find up-to-date information and additional links to support services for here.
source: Swiss Federal Office of Public Health

text: More about «Crans-Montana: useful information» Hazardous chemical products must carry a UFI code in Switzerland. In an emergency, medical professionals can use this code to quickly determine which substances are contained in the product and take targeted action.
source: Swiss Federal Office of Public Health

text: More about «The UFI code: rapid aid in an emergency» Protect yourself and others from respiratory infections Pathogens such as flu viruses, the coronavirus and the Respiratory syncytial virus (RSV) can cause respiratory infections. The symptoms of various respiratory diseases are similar. There is an increased risk of transmission and infection in indoor spaces where many people are gathered.
source: Swiss Federal Office of Public Health

text: How can we protect ourselves and others from respiratory infections? More about «Protect yourself and others from respiratory infections» Prices of medicines in Switzerland – FAQs Access to essential and innovative medicines is crucial for good-quality healthcare. At the same time, the system must remain affordable.
source: Swiss Federal Office of Public Health

text: In setting the prices of medicines, the FOPH has to evaluate the benefits of treatments and strike the right balance. More about «Prices of medicines in Switzerland – FAQs».
source: Swiss Federal Office of Public Health
